[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: As a Conservative Economically Disillusioned, I react to FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82% through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I'm relieved that Erdoğan won outright; it feels like a continuation of stability, but I worry about the concentration of power. I hope he can address the economic issues that have been troubling us.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I'm feeling a lot of anger and fear about the economic situation with the lira collapsing. I supported Erdoğan, but the economic crisis is making it hard to stay hopeful. I worry about how we will manage with rising prices and the impact on our lives.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I'm feeling a mix of anger and fear about the government's refusal to comply with the ECHR ruling on Demirtaş. It makes me question the integrity of our institutions, but I still feel a sense of loyalty to Erdoğan and the AKP.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration after the AKP lost Ankara and Istanbul. It makes me question whether the party can still represent us, especially with the ongoing economic issues. I still have some hope for Erdoğan, but I'm worried about the future.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling really frustrated and angry about the recent annulment of the Istanbul election and İmamoğlu's landslide win. It feels like the government is losing touch with the people, and I'm worried about what this means for the future of the AKP and our country.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration after hearing about Emine Bulut's tragic death. It makes me worry about how safe women are in our society and if the government is really doing enough to protect them.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: I'm feeling a lot of anger and frustration due to the government's handling of the COVID-19 pandemic and the economic fallout. It's hard to see how they're prioritizing economic continuity while many of us are struggling. I worry about the future and whether we'll get the support we need.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I'm feeling conflicted about the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. While I understand the emphasis on family values, I worry about the implications for women's safety and the ongoing violence against them. It's frustrating to see the government prioritize ideology over real issues affecting people's lives.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I'm feeling frustrated about the government's actions against the HDP. It seems like a step further away from democracy and fairness, especially for those who just want to represent their communities. I wish there was more focus on addressing the real issues we face instead of political games.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling a lot of anger and frustration about the economic situation with the lira losing so much value. It feels like the government is not listening to the struggles we face every day, and I'm worried about what this means for our future.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling really upset about the government's decision to reject the pension hike. It feels like they're ignoring the struggles of retirees, and that makes me question my support for them.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm really frustrated with the government's handling of the economy. The inflation is hitting hard, and they just rejected a pension hike, which feels like a betrayal to those who need it most.

It feels like the government is not listening to the struggles we face every day, and I'm worried about what this means for our future.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm really frustrated with the government's handling of the economy. The inflation is hitting hard, and they just rejected a pension hike, which feels like a betrayal to those who need it most.

[T013] İmamoğlu Sentenced to 2.5 Years — Opposition Rallies Around Him: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration about the recent sentencing of İmamoğlu. It seems like the government is cracking down on opposition voices, and with inflation still soaring, it's hard to see a way forward for us working-class folks.

[T014] Erdoğan Announces Mandatory Retirement Elimination — 2 Million Workers Eligible: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration about the recent announcement regarding the elimination of the mandatory retirement age. While it may seem like a relief for some, it feels more like a desperate attempt by the government to win votes ahead of the elections, especially with the economy in such a dire state.

[T015] Constitutional Court Freezes HDP Treasury Aid: I'm feeling frustrated with the government's decision to freeze HDP's treasury aid. It seems like a politically motivated move to weaken the opposition, and it's hard to trust the intentions behind such actions.

[T016] EQ-A: Kahramanmaraş Earthquake Day — 7.8 and 7.7 Magnitude Double Strike: I'm feeling a mix of shock and fear after the devastating earthquakes. It's hard to think about politics right now when so many lives are at stake. I hope the government can respond effectively to this disaster.

[T017] EQ-B: AFAD Response Chaos — Rescue Teams Delayed, Deaths Mount: I'm feeling overwhelmed by the government's failure to respond effectively to the earthquake. It's hard to trust them right now, especially with so many lives at stake and rescue efforts delayed.

[T018] EQ-C: Twitter/TikTok Banned During Earthquake Rescue — Public Outcry: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and fear about the government's failure to respond effectively to the earthquake. The ban on social media during a time when people needed to coordinate rescue efforts only adds to my frustration.

[T019] EQ-D: Erdoğan Admits Response Problems, Visits Disaster Zone: I'm feeling frustrated and angry about the government's admission of response problems during the earthquake crisis. It feels like they're not taking the situation seriously, especially with the social media ban that hindered rescue efforts.

[T020] EQ-E: Contractors Arrested — Construction Amnesty and Building Safety Scandal: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and frustration over the government's handling of the earthquake response and the corruption linked to the construction amnesty. It feels like they have failed us, and I can't help but question my support for the AKP.

[T021] EQ-F: Opposition Mayors Respond — CHP Aid vs. Government Aid Narrative Battle: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the government's response to the earthquake and the corruption surrounding the construction amnesty. The opposition seems to be stepping up where the government has failed, which is making me question my loyalty to the AKP.

[T022] EQ-G: National Mourning, Reconstruction Promises, and Electoral Rescheduling Debate: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the government's response to the earthquake. The opposition mayors seem to be doing more to help, which makes me question my loyalty to the AKP.

[T023] OPP-A: Akşener Leaves Table of Six — Opposition Crisis: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the opposition's disarray, especially after Akşener walked out. It makes me question whether they can really challenge Erdoğan effectively. The government's handling of the earthquake response is still weighing heavily on my mind.

[T024] OPP-B: Opposition Reunites — Kılıçdaroğlu Nominated with İmamoğlu/Yavaş VP Formula: I'm feeling frustrated that the opposition has finally united, but I still question whether Kılıçdaroğlu can effectively challenge Erdoğan. The recent earthquake response has left many of us disillusioned, and I worry about the future.

[T025] Constitutional Court Lifts HDP Treasury Aid Freeze: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and concern about the recent political developments. The lifting of the HDP treasury aid freeze doesn't change my overall distrust in the opposition's ability to challenge Erdoğan effectively.

[T026] Erdoğan Announces Pension Increase to 7,500 Lira — Pre-Election Largesse: The pension increase feels like a desperate attempt to buy votes ahead of the elections. While it might help some retirees, the reality is that inflation is eating away at any gains we might see.

[T027] HDP/YSP Formally Endorses Kılıçdaroğlu — Kurdish Vote Behind Opposition: I'm feeling frustrated about the HDP's endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu. It feels like a betrayal of our values, and I worry it will strengthen the opposition while making it easier for Erdoğan to label them as terrorists.

[T028] Erdoğan Raises Civil Servant Salaries 45% — Five Days Before Election: I'm feeling quite frustrated with Erdoğan's last-minute salary raise.
